text:
Market today: The mood is cautious, with cracks showing in the tech/chip sector. Concerns about the memory market topping out and a sell-off in AI-related debt are the real drivers, directly impacting your concentrated book. Geopolitical tensions are adding to overall market uncertainty.

Your holdings: SOXL (-35.06%) and SPAL (-23.1%) are both leveraged ETFs showing significant drawdowns and need immediate attention. QQQI (-2.16%) is also flagged for a trim with a clear stop. Remember, margin amplifies these drops.

Watch: Keep a close eye on the broader chip sector for signs of a top, and monitor AI funding news. Also, watch geopolitical developments as they can quickly shift market sentiment.

Bottom line: Prioritize managing risk in your leveraged, underperforming positions while staying alert to sector-specific and macro headwinds.
plays: Consider trimming SOXL given its deep -35.06% loss and chip sector concerns; respect its stop at 200.
Respect the critical stop at 21.38 on your new, leveraged SPAL position, currently down -23.1%.
RTX (defense) could be a new buy at 200.65-200.45 with a stop at 200.13, given escalating geopolitical tensions and scanner confirmation.
